data.description:
As U.S. commercial real estate investment is forecast to increase 16% to $562 billion in 2026, it becomes vital to pick the most suitable exchange-traded funds (ETFs) to invest in. Hedge fund investment activities can be a key gauge to guide investors.

Using the TipRanks Best Real Estate ETFs tool, this article identifies the iShares U.S. Real Estate ETF (IYR) as having received the best momentum from hedge funds in the recent quarter. The other two are the Vanguard Real Estate ETF (VNQ) and the Schwab U.S. REIT ETF (SCHH).

iShares U.S. Real Estate ETF (IYR)

IYR offers wide-ranging exposure to the U.S. real estate market via a blend of real estate investment trusts (REITs) alongside companies involved in property services and development activities. It manages roughly $3.95 billion in assets, the lowest asset base among the three ETFs discussed, and charges an expense ratio of 0.38%. 

TipRanks' Hedge Funds Trading Activity tool shows hedge funds expanded their holdings in this fund by about 467,000 shares in the last quarter. However, like the rest of the funds in this article, the confidence signal on IYR is Neutral.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Microsoft Stock (NASDAQ:MSFT) Slips as its Prominence in the Carbon Removal Market Emerges
data.description:
Remember when carbon credits were a big deal? Tech giant Microsoft (MSFT) does, and actually has been working as though they still are a big deal. New reports have emerged that suggest Microsoft is effectively the entire carbon removal market by itself right now, having purchased fully 93% of global carbon removals. Investors were less than pleased, as evidenced by a fractional drop in share prices in Friday afternoon's trading.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The data emerged as part of the Sustainable Energy in America Factbook, a publication from BloombergNEF and the Business Council for Sustainable Energy. This is also the first time that the groups have tracked carbon removal market movements, which led to the discovery that most of that market is one company.

Microsoft made multiple long-term deals, and represents an "anchor buyer" in the sector, the report noted. This funding is actually helping other companies ramp up their own practices for carbon removal, which will eventually send prices down as more competitors enter the field. Microsoft bought credits enough to remove 52.35 million metric tons of CO2e from the air. The second-largest buyer, an undisclosed firm, removed 1.89 million.

The Red Ring of Death Revealed

While the infamous Red Ring of Death has not been a phenomenon for years now, ever since the Xbox 360 stopped being the primary platform, it still sends a chill down the spines of Xbox gamers who likely remember the event. New reports detail just what the Red Ring of Death was, and how it came to be.

Most believed it was an issue of overheating. This was partially true, but according to hardware engineer Leo Del Castillo, it was caused by the console getting very hot, and then rapidly cooling off. This combination of high heat followed by room temperature conditions proved extremely stressful, and ended up impacting the connection between the GPU and the motherboard.

Is Microsoft a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on MSFT stock based on 33 Buys and three Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 2.39% loss in its share price over the past year, the average MSFT price target of $594.02 per share implies 49.56% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Polymarket Announces Exclusive Partnership with Substack as IPO Talks Spread
data.description:
The world's largest prediction market, Polymarket, has partnered with Substack, a U.S.-based newsletter and publishing platform, to let writers display live prediction market data directly in their posts. The integration provides dynamic probability tables that update in real time for elections, AI developments, economic trends, and entertainment events. 

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Meanwhile, Polymarket remains privately held, though market watchers have reported speculation about a potential initial public offering (IPO).

Polymarket Expands Reach Through Substack Partnership 

On Feb. 18, Polymarket announced the partnership on X, describing it as an exclusive collaboration with Substack. The company said that, starting immediately, Substack authors can natively integrate its prediction data directly into their publications. In the same post, Polymarket stated, "Journalism is better when it's backed by live markets."

Following the rollout, Chris Best, co-founder of Substack, reported that 1 in 5 of its top 250 highest-revenue publications have begun using Polymarket data. The integration places live market pricing directly inside independent newsletters, allowing readers to track event probabilities alongside written analysis. As a result, the partnership extends Polymarket's data distribution beyond its trading platform and into digital media channels.

Meanwhile, the partnership comes amid regulatory scrutiny, with Mike Selig, Chair of the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC), stating he plans to challenge state laws and litigation that could impose restrictions on prediction markets. While Polymarket has not announced formal IPO plans or filed for an offering, analysts have pointed to its reported multi-billion-dollar valuation and institutional interest as factors that could support a future listing. 

Polymarket Expands Across Media and Crypto

Polymarket has not only moved into the media industry but has also hinted at plans to expand its footprint in the crypto sector. The private company filed U.S. trademark applications for the "POLY" and "$POLY" earlier this February, currently pending with the United States Patent and Trademark Office. The filings cover multiple classes, including downloadable software for financial and cryptocurrency trading, digital token services, and platform-as-a-service offerings for electronic trading. 

While no official launch date has been announced, earlier statements from executives indicate plans to introduce a native token alongside a future U.S. app relaunch. 

Can I invest in Polymarket Shares?

Polymarket is privately held, so its shares are not available for purchase. The prediction company has also not announced any definite IPO plans. However, investors can track its performance and that of other private companies via TipRanks private company center.
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: Judgment Sticks: Tesla Stock (NASDAQ:TSLA) Slips as Tesla on the Hook for $243 Million
data.description:
Bad news hit for electric vehicle giant Tesla (TSLA), as a federal judge stepped in to keep a $243 million jury award against Tesla over an Autopilot-related crash. Tesla's hopes of getting the verdict set aside were dashed, at least for now, and a nine-figure bill is now in Tesla's future. The verdict did little damage to shareholder spirits, as measured by the fractional loss in Tesla shares seen in Friday afternoon's trading.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The word came down from United States District Judge Beth Bloom that the evidence presented so far "…more than supported" the verdict as laid out. Tesla, meanwhile, offered no new arguments that would prompt a second look. This effectively runs Tesla out of options at the trial court level. Given that Tesla rejected a settlement offer before trial that would have cost less than a quarter of the $243 million figure, it is a decision that likely still haunts the company.

Tesla, back in August, tried to advance the notion that the verdict itself "…flies in the face of basic Florida tort law, the Due Process Clause, and common sense." But Judge Bloom found the response lacking, and Tesla suggested that it would take the verdict to a higher court, further noting a pre-trial judgment that would cap punitive damages at three times what compensatory damages would be. This would reduce the payout, but would still leave it at the nine-figure mark.

No Longer Taking Reviewer Calls

Meanwhile, Tesla seems to have cut ties with one particular tech reviewer and YouTube personality, Marques Brownlee. Otherwise known as MKBHD, Brownlee reviewed the Model Y Performance recently, but had to get it on his own, as Tesla would no longer talk to him. Normally, a car company offering review products will offer one for a week or so on loan. But Brownlee found himself out in the cold with Tesla.

Reports suggest that Tesla's cold reception of Brownlee came from a 2023 event in which he talked about the Cybertruck. Brownlee declared that Tesla was "…straight up not delivering" on promises. This was made worse by Brownlee publicly canceling his order for two Tesla Roadsters, which hit Tesla accordingly.

Is Tesla a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 21.88% rally in its share price over the past year, the average TSLA price target of $396.80 per share implies 3.2% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Trump Tariff Refund Process Is 'Likely to be a Mess,' Warns SCOTUS
data.description:
On Friday, the Supreme Court ruled 6-3 that President Trump overstepped his legal authority by imposing sweeping global tariffs. The decision could put $175 billion of tariff refunds on the table.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The ruling noted that the "Court says nothing today about whether, and if so how, the Government should go about returning the billions of dollars" of tariff revenue. At the same time, SCOTUS cautioned that a potential refund process "is likely to be a 'mess,' as was acknowledged at oral
arguments."

Trump Turns to Backup Plan to Enforce Tariffs

Refunding tariff revenue could require another SCOTUS ruling and will likely be a lengthy process. Over the course of 2025, the average U.S. import tariff rate rose to 13% from 2.6%.

Trump characterized Friday's ruling as a "disgrace" and reiterated that he has a backup plan to enforce tariffs. That could include Section 301 and Section 122 of the Trade Act of 1974, which allow the president to impose tariffs or trade restrictions to address unfair trade practices or protect domestic industries.

data.title: Cathie Wood Scoops Up $24M in Figma, Sells DraftKings
data.description:
Cathie Wood's ARK Invest ETFs (exchange-traded funds) made notable portfolio changes on Thursday, February 19, as shown in daily fund disclosures. Wood bought roughly $24 million worth of Figma (FIG) stock, following its robust Q4FY25 results. Her purchase reflects solid confidence in the newly listed cloud-based collaborative design platform's future prospects. She also acquired shares of British drug maker Compass Pathways (CMPS) and biotech firm Recursion Pharmaceuticals (RXRX).

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

On the other hand, Wood offloaded a large portion of betting platform DraftKings (DKNG) and social media site Pinterest (PINS). Let's briefly review Wood's trades:

Figma – Wood added 964,342 shares, while its share price surged 6.9% yesterday. Figma beat both sales and earnings estimates, and CEO Dylan Field pushed back on fears that generative AI tools could slow the growth of traditional software companies. The company is also leveraging AI to boost its platform. Its Figma Make tool lets users type prompts that AI models from Anthropic and Google (GOOGL) turn into app prototypes. Starting in March, Figma will charge for AI usage through monthly credit limits and pay-per-use pricing.

Compass Pathways – The ARK Genomic Revolution ETF (ARKG) purchased 781,085 shares for $6.5 million, capitalizing on the 2.3% dip in share price. Shares fell after announcing a 17.5 million secondary offering priced at $8 per piece.

Recursion Pharmaceuticals – Wood bought 780,532 RXRX shares for $2.9 million, following a 5.4% surge in its stock price. The surge follows the prior day's decline, when Nvidia (NVDA) disclosed in a 13F filing that it had fully exited its position in the AI-driven drug maker during Q4.  

DraftKings – The ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK) sold 480,919 shares for about $10.8 million, continuing with her steady exit from the gaming platform.

Pinterest – The ARK Next Generation Internet ETF (ARKW) sold 158,945 shares for $2.66 million, aligning with her recent selling spree after it reported lackluster Q4 2025 results and issued a weak outlook.

Wood's Strategic Portfolio Shuffle

Let's see how these stocks perform using the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool.

Currently, analysts have a "Strong Buy" consensus rating on DraftKings and Compass Pathways, with CMPS stock offering the highest upside potential over the next twelve months.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Dow Jones Today: DJIA Edges Higher as SCOTUS Strikes Down Trump's Tariffs; GDP Falls Short
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is trading slightly higher on Friday after the Supreme Court issued a ruling on the legality of President Trump's tariffs alongside a GDP update from the Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA).

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In a 6-3 ruling, SCOTUS ruled that Trump's tariffs are illegal and said he overstepped his authority when using the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA) to enforce them. The act allows the president to impose economic sanctions, such as tariffs, on foreign nations to address national emergencies.

"The president asserts the extraordinary power to unilaterally impose tariffs of unlimited amount, duration, and scope," wrote Chief Justice John Roberts. "In light of the breadth, history, and constitutional context of that asserted authority, he must identify clear congressional authorization to exercise it."

However, SCOTUS didn't provide guidance on a tariff refund process and warned that it "is likely to be a 'mess,' as was acknowledged at oral arguments." The U.S. could be on the hook for $175 billion of refunds, according to Penn-Wharton Budget Model economists.

Trump labeled the ruling as a "disgrace" and reiterated that he has a backup plan. The plan could include Section 122 of the Trade Act of 1974, which allows tariffs of up to 15% for a maximum of 150 days. Trump may also turn to Section 338 of the Smoot-Hawley Tariff Act, which permits tariffs of up to 50% on countries identified as engaging in unfair trade practices with the U.S., according to Capital Economics Chief North America economist Paul Ashworth.

Meanwhile, the BEA announced this morning that U.S. gross domestic product (GDP) grew at an annualized rate of 1.4% during the fourth quarter, below the estimate of 3% and down from 4.4% in the prior quarter. Consumer spending and investment boosted GDP, while the effects of the government shutdown and declining exports weighed it down.

The Dow Jones is up by 0.07% at the time of writing.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: "Green Light from the DOJ?" Paramount (PSKY) Clears Antitrust Review as It Looks to Disrupt WBD-Netflix Merger
data.description:
Paramount Skydance (PSKY) has cleared a major U.S. regulatory milestone in its push to acquire Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD), saying it now faces "no statutory impediment" in the U.S. to closing its proposed $77.9 billion takeover. The development gives Paramount added momentum as it tries to disrupt WBD's existing agreement to sell its studio and streaming assets to Netflix (NFLX).

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In an SEC filing, the company said the 10-day waiting period under the Hart‑Scott‑Rodino (HSR) Act expired on February 19, signaling that the U.S. Department of Justice has completed its Second Request review without taking action to block the deal.

While this does not amount to formal approval, it removes a key early hurdle and suggests the DOJ does not currently see antitrust issues with a potential Paramount-WBD merger.

Race for WBD Heats Up

The regulatory progress comes as Paramount and Netflix remain locked in a high‑stakes bidding battle for WBD. Netflix holds a signed $83 billion agreement to acquire WBD's studio and streaming operations, but WBD's board agreed to reopen talks with Paramount after receiving a revised hostile bid. Paramount has indicated it is willing to raise its offer by $1 per share, bringing the total to $31.

Currently, WBD maintains its recommendation for the Netflix deal. Also, a special shareholder meeting is set for March 20 to vote on the Netflix merger and a spin-off of WBD's cable assets.

Paramount Still Faces a Long Road to a Deal

Even with the U.S. antitrust review moving in its favor, Paramount still faces several hurdles. It must negotiate and sign a definitive merger agreement with WBD and secure shareholder approval.

Also, it must obtain regulatory clearance in other major jurisdictions, including the European Union and the United Kingdom. Paramount noted it has already received approval from Germany's foreign investment authorities.

With WBD's seven‑day deadline approaching, Paramount now has limited time to deliver its best and final offer, setting the stage for an even more intense bidding battle.

Is Paramount Stock a Good Buy?

On TipRanks, PSKY stock has a Moderate Sell consensus rating based on one Hold and three Sell ratings. The average Paramount Skydance price target of $12.33 implies 13.85% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, PSKY shares have dropped 4.8%.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Trump's Tariffs Struck Down by Supreme Court in 6-3 Ruling
data.description:
The Supreme Court announced Friday that it had struck down President Trump's tariffs in a 6‑3 ruling. SCOTUS said Trump exceeded his executive power when invoking the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA) to enforce sweeping "reciprocal" tariffs. The act allows the president to impose economic sanctions, including tariffs, on foreign nations in response to declared national emergencies.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"IEEPA does not authorize the President to impose tariffs," read the ruling.

Supreme Court Ruling Puts $175 Billion of Tariff Refunds on the Line

In February 2025, Trump imposed tariffs on Canada, China and Mexico for failing to prevent the flow of fentanyl in the U.S. In April, he imposed 10% tariffs on nearly all countries, with higher rates targeting those deemed to engage in unfair trade practices with the U.S.

Since then, the U.S. has collected $175 billion of tariff revenue, according to an estimate from Penn-Wharton Budget Model economists. In other words, $175 billion of tariff refunds could be up for grabs.

However, the ruling noted that the "Court says nothing today about whether, and if so how, the Government should go about returning the billions of dollars that it has collected from importers." This complicates any potential refund process and could require another ruling.

data.author: Eddie Pan
